sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Level 1 completed! The key was clicking the lower blue block 6 times to shift the scene left until the charcoal object moved to a specific position (cols 10-13). The pink bar also reset to 64.
Now I'm on Level 2. Let me analyze the new level and apply what I learned:
- The goal is to shift the scene using blue blocks until the charcoal object reaches a specific position
- I need to find the blue blocks and charcoal object in the new layout
Let me inspect the current level.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Analyze Level 2
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Level 2 objects:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see Level 2. Let me analyze:
- Pink bar: 64 pixels (full)
- Blue blocks (b): at cols 0-3, multiple rows (16-19, 24-27, 36-39, 44-47)
- Charcoal object (c): at rows 52-57, cols 10-13
- Light green objects (N): at rows 40-43, cols 28-29 and rows 52-57, cols 8-9
- Black bars (B): at rows 20-23, cols 0-55 and rows 40-43, cols 0-27 and 30-43
- White area: rows 1-19, cols 0-51 and rows 24-39, cols 0-11 and rows 44-63, cols 0-7
- Gray area: rows 1-63, cols 8-63
The blue blocks are on the LEFT side (cols 0-3) this time, not the right. There are 4 blue blocks at different vertical positions.
The charcoal object is at cols 10-13.
The light green objects might be analogous to the yellow objects in Level 1.
In Level 1, I needed to shift the scene LEFT by clicking the lower blue block. The scene shifted until the charcoal reached cols 10-13, which triggered level completion.
In Level 2, the charcoal is already at cols 10-13! But the level hasn't completed yet. So maybe the goal is different, or maybe I need to shift the scene to a different position.
Wait, in Level 1, the charcoal ended up at cols 10-13 after 6 left shifts. In Level 2, the charcoal starts at cols 10-13. So maybe I need to shift the scene RIGHT this time?
Let me think about the blue blocks. There are 4 blue blocks at different vertical positions. In Level 1, the upper blue block shifted right and the lower blue block shifted left. Here, maybe different blue blocks have different effects.
Let me try clicking the topmost blue block (rows 16-19, cols 0-3) to see what happens.

sections.content:

<function=python>

action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 17, 'col': 1}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("After clicking top blue block:")
for n in seg['nodes']:

    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")

</function>
